project.name: TUGAS-AKHIR-VAR-TATA-SURYA
project.currentFocus: Stabilize mobile AR close/reopen viewport and normalize camera preview scale using visualViewport-driven app height.

lastAchievements: Phase 0 baseline and repeated follow-up builds passed during AR viewport stabilization.
Phase 1 cleanup: AR viewport sync now runs only while scanner is active and delayed cleanup removes late AR.js artifacts after close.
Reverted harmful body-lock behavior after real-device feedback showed Android Chrome could zoom out into tiled pages.
Added visualViewport-driven --app-height and removed scanner 100dvh/calc(--vh) dependency so AR.js no longer inherits huge layout heights such as 375x2669.
Changed AR video presentation to contain for normal 1x/no-crop camera preview instead of cover-driven zoom/crop.
Kept zoom-min reset path and GLB Sun node downscale so camera preview and model size remain controlled on phones.
pendingIssues: Current viewport/camera patch still needs real-device confirmation on Android Chrome because camera streams and visualViewport behavior differ by browser/device.
object-fit contain avoids camera crop/zoom but may show black bars when the browser supplies a landscape camera stream inside a portrait screen.
If the camera still appears digitally zoomed after this patch, the next safe step is a custom getUserMedia AR source with explicit constraints instead of relying fully on AR.js webcam setup.
If tap hit-zones drift after final marker scale, hitZonePosition/hitZoneRadius tuning is still required.
nextBestActions: Deploy and test on phone: open AR, close scanner, and confirm landing returns full-size instead of tiny top-left.
Check DevTools after reopen: AR.js actual source dimensions should no longer report a huge height like 2669 for a 375px-wide viewport.
Scan Hiro marker and verify camera framing, Sun size, and planet tap hit-zones.
If the user wants full portrait fill again, add a controlled preview mode toggle instead of reverting globally to cover.
validationState: npm run build after visualViewport/app-height camera patch: PASS
git diff --check after visualViewport/app-height camera patch: PASS with Git line-ending warnings only

designContinuity.currentConceptualAnchor: Handheld optical instrument panel (camera-first scanner HUD).
